description: Income from carrying on business or profession. Two paths: (1) Presumptive income (Sections 44AD/44ADA/44AE — flat % basis, ITR-4) or (2) Actual income with books of accounts (ITR-3). Most significant FY 2025-26 changes: ITR-4 now requires mandatory cash/bank balance disclosure in Schedule BP.

oldRegime.rate: Slab rate on business income after all allowable deductions. Old regime requires Form 10-IEA if taxpayer has business income.
oldRegime.notes: Old regime requires Form 10-IEA filing for business income taxpayers

newRegime.rate: Slab rate — new regime is default. No HRA, 80C, 80D deductions. Only 80CCD(2) and standard deduction allowed.

incomeComponents.name: Presumptive Income u/s 44AD (Eligible Business)
incomeComponents.description: 8% of turnover (or 6% for digital receipts) declared as income without maintaining full books. Turnover limit: ₹3 Crore (₹2 Crore if cash > 5%). For retail traders, manufacturers, transporters (not professionals).

incomeComponents.name: Presumptive Income u/s 44ADA (Eligible Professionals)
incomeComponents.description: 50% of gross receipts declared as income. For resident individuals who are doctors, lawyers, CAs, engineers, architects, interior designers, technical consultants. Gross receipts limit: ₹75L (₹50L if cash > 5%).

incomeComponents.name: Presumptive Income u/s 44AE (Goods Transport)
incomeComponents.description: Per vehicle income: Heavy goods vehicles (GVW > 12T): ₹1,000/ton of GVW/month; Other vehicles: ₹7,500/month. Owner must not own more than 10 vehicles at any time during the year.

incomeComponents.name: Actual Business Income (Books of Accounts)
incomeComponents.description: Actual net profit from P&L account after all allowable deductions. Used when not under presumptive scheme or when turnover exceeds presumptive limits.

deductionsAvailable.name: Business Expenses (General)
deductionsAvailable.limit: Actual amount — must be genuinely for business
deductionsAvailable.notes: Expenditure not personal, not capital, wholly for business. 40A(3) disallows cash payments > ₹10K.

deductionsAvailable.name: Depreciation (IT Act WDV method)
deductionsAvailable.notes: Separate from book depreciation. 50% rate for assets acquired in second half of year.

deductionsAvailable.name: Employee Contribution to PF/ESI
deductionsAvailable.limit: Actual — if deposited within due dates
deductionsAvailable.notes: Only deductible if deposited by due date; otherwise disallowed (Kessoram Industries ruling)

deductionsAvailable.name: Employer NPS contribution
deductionsAvailable.notes: Available even in new regime. Raised from 10% in FY 2025-26.

keyRules.rule: Tax audit u/s 44AB mandatory when: Business turnover > ₹1Cr (₹10Cr if digital-heavy — cash receipts and payments ≤ 5% of total). Professional receipts > ₹50L. Opted out of 44AD with income below minimum rate.
44AD 5-year lock-in: Once a taxpayer opts out of 44AD (declares income < 8% or maintains books), they cannot use 44AD for the next 5 consecutive assessment years.
GST reconciliation: GSTR-9 annual return turnover is cross-matched with ITR turnover. Significant discrepancy triggers AI-based notices. Reconcile before filing.
Schedule BP mandatory cash/bank balance (FY 2025-26): ITR-4 filers under 44AD/44ADA/44AE MUST disclose actual cash in hand and aggregate bank balance as on March 31, 2026. New mandatory field.
keyRules.legalReference: Schedule BP, ITR-4 Form for AY 2026-27

keyRules.rule: Old regime with business income: Taxpayer must file Form 10-IEA before or with ITR to opt for old tax regime. Failure means new regime is applied by default.

commonMistakes: Declaring income below presumptive minimum without maintaining actual books (invalid — must use ITR-3 with books)
Re-using 44AD within 5-year lock-in period after opting out
Not reconciling GST turnover with ITR turnover
Claiming personal expenses as business expenses u/s 37
Not disclosing cash/bank balance in ITR-4 Schedule BP (NEW mandatory for FY 2025-26)
Cash payment > ₹10K claimed as deduction (disallowed u/s 40A(3))
auditTriggers: Low GP ratio compared to industry average or prior years
Cash sales percentage higher than industry norm
Large purchases without corresponding GST input credits
Significant deposits in bank not matching declared business income
Turnaround from losses to profit or vice versa without explanation
